# ResultCrafter

> A minimal, opinionated Result pattern library for modern .NET (8+). Provides `Result<T>`, `Result`, and `Error`
> as `readonly struct` types, with built-in RFC 9457 ProblemDetails pipeline, structured logging via
> `[LoggerMessage]`, first-class Minimal API support, and full MVC controller support.
> Ships as five focused NuGet packages under the `ResultCrafter.*` prefix.

## Key design decisions

- `Result<T>`, `Result`, and `Error` are `readonly struct` — zero heap allocation on the success path.
- `ErrorType` enum starts at `None = 0` (sentinel). All factory methods on `Error` reject `None`, so
  `default(Error)` and `default(ErrorType)` are explicitly invalid rather than silently mapping to BadRequest.
- Both `Result<T>` and void `Result` carry a `SuccessKind` field (`Ok`, `Created`, `Accepted`, `NoContent`).
  The `To*Result()` extension methods validate that the `SuccessKind` matches the intended HTTP status code
  and throw `InvalidOperationException` on mismatch.
- `Result<T>.Map<TOut>(Func<T, TOut>)` transforms the success value while preserving `SuccessKind` and
  `Location`. On failure, the error propagates unchanged without invoking the selector.
- Error responses route through `IProblemDetailsService` on both the Minimal API and controller paths, so enrichment
  (instance, traceId, requestId) and 4xx structured logging fire from a single `IPostConfigureOptions` callback —
  `ConfigureResultCrafterProblemDetails` — with no per-path wiring.
- `ProblemDetailsBuilder` sets the RFC 9110 `type` URI explicitly via `HttpErrorCatalog.TypeUri()`, so the
  ProblemDetails object is self-describing at all stages of the pipeline.
- The exception handler uses `IExceptionHandler` (not custom middleware) so the try/catch cost is paid only when an
  exception actually occurs, not on every request.
- All log calls use `[LoggerMessage]` source generation; the 4xx path has an explicit `IsEnabled` guard to avoid
  string allocations when the log level is filtered.
- `ProblemActionResult` (controller path) calls `IProblemDetailsService.WriteAsync` inside `ExecuteResultAsync`,
  giving controllers identical pipeline behaviour to Minimal API endpoints.
- `GetInstance` returns `PathBase + Path` only (a valid RFC 9457 relative URI reference). The host is intentionally
  excluded — it breaks behind reverse proxies.
- `EfCoreConcurrencyExceptionHandler` is inserted *before* `ResultCrafterExceptionHandler` in the DI list so
  `DbUpdateConcurrencyException` resolves as 409 rather than falling through to 500.
- `ResultCrafterMarker` + `ResultCrafterStartupValidator` enforce that both `AddResultCrafter()` and
  `UseResultCrafter()` are called; misconfiguration throws at startup, not at the first request.
- `ResultCrafter.MediatR` provides two open-generic MediatR behaviors (`Result<T>` and void `Result`) because
  `IPipelineBehavior` requires an exact response-type match; a single behavior cannot cover both without reflection.
- MediatR validation behaviors execute validators sequentially (not `Task.WhenAll`) for safety with validators that
  depend on non-thread-safe scoped services (e.g. EF Core `DbContext`); failures are aggregated into one field-errors
  dictionary.
- `HttpErrorCatalog` switch expressions throw `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` on unrecognised `ErrorType` values
  (including `None`) rather than silently falling back to a default.

## Packages

- `ResultCrafter.Core` — primitives only, no framework dependencies.
- `ResultCrafter.AspNetCore` — ProblemDetails pipeline, exception handler, Minimal API extensions, MVC controller
   extensions. Requires .NET 8+. Multi-targets net8.0;net9.0;net10.0.
- `ResultCrafter.AspNetCore.EfCore` — intercepts `DbUpdateConcurrencyException` -> 409. Call
  `AddResultCrafterEfCore()` after `AddResultCrafter()`.
- `ResultCrafter.FluentValidation` — `ValidateToResultAsync` bridge from `IValidator<T>` to `Error.BadRequest`.
- `ResultCrafter.MediatR` — MediatR pipeline behaviors that run FluentValidation automatically for handlers returning
  `Result` / `Result<T>`, short-circuiting with `Error.BadRequest(fieldErrors)`.

## Source layout

### Core primitives (`src/ResultCrafter.Core/`)

- [Error.cs](src/ResultCrafter.Core/Primitives/Error.cs): `readonly struct`. Constructor rejects `ErrorType.None`.
  Factory methods: `NotFound`, `Unauthorized`, `Forbidden`, `Conflict`, `ConcurrencyConflict`,
  `BadRequest(string)`, `BadRequest(Dictionary<string,string[]>)`. `IsValidation` is true only when constructed
  with a field-errors dict.
- [Result.cs](src/ResultCrafter.Core/Primitives/Result.cs): Void result with `SuccessKind` field. Factories:
  `NoContent` (Kind=NoContent), `Accepted` (Kind=Accepted), `Fail`. Implicit cast from `Error`.
- [ResultT.cs](src/ResultCrafter.Core/Primitives/ResultT.cs): `Result<T>` with `SuccessKind` field. Factories:
  `Ok`, `Created`, `Accepted`, `Fail`. Implicit cast from `T` and from `Error`.
  `Map<TOut>(Func<T, TOut>)` transforms the success value preserving Kind and Location.
- [ErrorType.cs](src/ResultCrafter.Core/Primitives/ErrorType.cs): `None = 0` (sentinel), `BadRequest = 1`,
  `NotFound = 2`, `Conflict = 3`, `Unauthorized = 4`, `Forbidden = 5`, `ConcurrencyConflict = 6`.
- [SuccessKind.cs](src/ResultCrafter.Core/Primitives/SuccessKind.cs): `Ok`, `Created`, `Accepted`, `NoContent`.

### ASP.NET Core — DI wiring (`src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore/DependencyInjection/`)

- [ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs](src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore/DependencyInjection/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs):
  `AddResultCrafter()` — registers `AddProblemDetails`, `IPostConfigureOptions`, `IExceptionHandler`,
  `ResultCrafterMarker`, `ResultCrafterStartupValidator`.
- [ApplicationBuilderExtensions.cs](src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore/DependencyInjection/ApplicationBuilderExtensions.cs):
  `UseResultCrafter()` — calls `UseExceptionHandler()` + `UseStatusCodePages()`, marks the sentinel.
- [ConfigureResultCrafterProblemDetails.cs](src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore/DependencyInjection/ConfigureResultCrafterProblemDetails.cs):
  `IPostConfigureOptions<ProblemDetailsOptions>` — enriches every response, logs 4xx, strips internal `x-rc`
  marker extensions.
- [ResultCrafterMarker.cs](src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore/DependencyInjection/ResultCrafterMarker.cs): Sentinel that
  tracks whether `UseResultCrafter()` was called.
- [ResultCrafterStartupValidator.cs](src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore/DependencyInjection/ResultCrafterStartupValidator.cs):
  `IHostedService` — throws `InvalidOperationException` at startup if the middleware was not registered.

### ASP.NET Core — Minimal API (`src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore/Endpoints/`)

- [MinimalApiResultExtensions.cs](src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore/Endpoints/MinimalApiResultExtensions.cs):
  `ToOkResult`, `ToCreatedResult`, `ToAcceptedResult`, `ToNoContentResult`, `ToProblemResult` — all return
  `Results<TSuccess, ProblemHttpResult>` typed unions for OpenAPI inference. `ToNoContentResult` validates
  `Kind == NoContent`; `ToAcceptedResult` validates `Kind == Accepted`; `ToCreatedResult` validates
  `Kind == Created`.
- [RouteHandlerBuilderExtensions.cs](...):
   `.ProducesBadRequest()`, `.ProducesUnauthorized()`, `.ProducesForbidden()`, `.ProducesNotFound()`,
   `.ProducesConflict()` — OpenAPI documentation only, no runtime effect.

### ASP.NET Core — MVC controllers (`src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore/Controllers/`)

- [ControllerResultExtensions.cs](src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore/Controllers/ControllerResultExtensions.cs):
  `ToOkResult`, `ToCreatedResult`, `ToAcceptedResult`, `ToNoContentResult`, `ToProblemResult` — returns
  `ActionResult<T>` / `IActionResult`. Failure path returns `ProblemActionResult`. Same `SuccessKind`
  validation as Minimal API path.
- [ProblemActionResult.cs](...): `ActionResult` subclass. `ExecuteResultAsync` calls
  `IProblemDetailsService.WriteAsync` (no fallback — a writer is always present because
  `AddResultCrafter()` guarantees `AddProblemDetails()` is registered). Enrichment, 4xx
   logging, and internal marker-extension stripping fire from `ConfigureResultCrafterProblemDetails`
   identically to the Minimal API path. No `Clone` helper or manual JSON fallback exists.
- [ControllerResultAttributes.cs](src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore/Controllers/ControllerResultAttributes.cs):
  `[ProducesBadRequest]`, `[ProducesUnauthorized]`, `[ProducesForbidden]`, `[ProducesNotFound]`,
  `[ProducesConflict]` — each inherits `ProducesResponseTypeAttribute<ProblemDetails>`, `AllowMultiple = true`.

### ASP.NET Core — ProblemDetails utilities (`src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore/ProblemDetails/`)

- [ProblemDetailsBuilder.cs](src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore/ProblemDetails/ProblemDetailsBuilder.cs): Builds
  `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.ProblemDetails` from `Error`. Sets `Type` (RFC 9110 URI), `x-rc` marker and
  `x-rc-error-id` extension keys; adds `errors` dict only when `error.IsValidation`.
- [HttpErrorCatalog.cs](src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore/ProblemDetails/HttpErrorCatalog.cs): `Status(ErrorType)`,
  `Title(ErrorType)`, `DefaultDetail(ErrorType)`, `TypeUri(ErrorType)`, `ResolveDetail(Error)` — pure switch
  expressions, no reflection. All throw `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` on `ErrorType.None`.
- [ProblemDetailsEnricher.cs](src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore/ProblemDetails/ProblemDetailsEnricher.cs):
  `GetInstance(HttpContext)` -> `PathBase + Path` (relative URI, host excluded). `GetTraceId(HttpContext)` -> W3C
  activity ID. Both cached in `HttpContext.Items` with typed object keys. `Enrich(pd, ctx)` — does not overwrite
  existing values.
- [ProblemDetailsKeys.cs](src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore/ProblemDetails/ProblemDetailsKeys.cs): `x-rc` (marker,
  stripped before response), `x-rc-error-id` (error type string, stripped before response), `errors`
  (field-errors dict, kept in response).
- [ResultCrafterLogger.cs](src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore/ProblemDetails/ResultCrafterLogger.cs):
  `[LoggerMessage]` source-generated methods: `LogClientError` (level param, EventId 1), `LogUnhandledException`
  (Error level, EventId 2), `LogResponseAlreadyStarted` (Warning level, EventId 3).

### ASP.NET Core — Exception handling (`src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore/ExceptionHandling/`)

- [ResultCrafterExceptionHandler.cs](src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore/ExceptionHandling/ResultCrafterExceptionHandler.cs):
  `IExceptionHandler` — catches all unhandled exceptions, logs at Error, writes 500 ProblemDetails. Detail is
  sanitised in production (`ExceptionDetailMode.Auto` checks env name at startup using
  `OrdinalIgnoreCase` comparisons). Returns `false` if response has already started.

### ASP.NET Core — Options (`src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore/Options/`)

- [ResultCrafterOptions.cs](src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore/Options/ResultCrafterOptions.cs): `ExceptionDetailMode`
  (Auto/Sanitized/IncludeExceptionDetails), `DefaultServerErrorMessage`, `ClientErrorLogLevel`.

### EF Core integration (`src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore.EfCore/`)

- [EfCoreConcurrencyExceptionHandler.cs](src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore.EfCore/EfCoreConcurrencyExceptionHandler.cs):
  Handles `DbUpdateConcurrencyException` -> 409. Inserted before `ResultCrafterExceptionHandler` in the DI
  registration list.
- [ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs](src/ResultCrafter.AspNetCore.EfCore/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs):
  `AddResultCrafterEfCore()` — locates the existing `ResultCrafterExceptionHandler` descriptor and inserts the EF
  Core handler before it. Throws if `AddResultCrafter()` was not called first.

### FluentValidation integration (`src/ResultCrafter.FluentValidation/`)

- [ValidatorExtensions.cs](src/ResultCrafter.FluentValidation/ValidatorExtensions.cs):
  `ValidateToResultAsync<T>` — returns `null` on success, `Error?` (a `BadRequest` with field-errors dict) on
  failure. Property names come from FluentValidation as-is; configure
  `ValidatorOptions.Global.PropertyNameResolver` for casing conventions.

### MediatR integration (`src/ResultCrafter.MediatR/`)

- [ResultValidationBehavior.cs](src/ResultCrafter.MediatR/ResultValidationBehavior.cs):
  `IPipelineBehavior<TRequest, Result<T>>` — runs all registered `IValidator<TRequest>` instances before the handler,
  aggregates failures, returns `Result<T>.Fail(Error.BadRequest(fieldErrors))` on validation failure.
  Empty `PropertyName` is normalised to `"_"` using `string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace`.
- [VoidResultValidationBehavior.cs](src/ResultCrafter.MediatR/VoidResultValidationBehavior.cs):
  `IPipelineBehavior<TRequest, Result>` — same validation behavior for handlers returning void `Result`.
- [ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs](src/ResultCrafter.MediatR/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs):
  `AddResultCrafterValidation(this MediatRServiceConfiguration)` — registers both open behaviors with MediatR.

### Demo project (`tests/ResultCrafter.Demo/`)

- [Program.cs](tests/ResultCrafter.Demo/Program.cs): Wires `AddResultCrafter`, `AddResultCrafterEfCore`,
  `AddControllers`, `ItemService` (Singleton), validators as Singleton. Mounts Minimal API group at `/api` and
  controllers at `/api/ctrl`.
- [ItemEndpoints.cs](tests/ResultCrafter.Demo/ItemEndpoints.cs): Minimal API endpoints covering all success kinds
  (Ok, Created, Accepted typed/void, NoContent) and all error types. Includes `/items/crash`
  (unhandled exception -> 500) and `/items/db-crash` (`DbUpdateConcurrencyException` -> 409).
- [ItemsController.cs](tests/ResultCrafter.Demo/ItemsController.cs): MVC controller mirror
  of the Minimal API endpoints under `/api/ctrl/items`. Same `ItemService`, same result methods, attribute-based
  OpenAPI documentation. Includes `Crash()` and `DbCrash()` actions.
- [ItemService.cs](tests/ResultCrafter.Demo/ItemService.cs): Thread-safe in-memory service using
  `ConcurrentDictionary` and `Interlocked.Increment`, exercising every Result/Error path.
- [ResultCrafter.Demo.http](tests/ResultCrafter.Demo/ResultCrafter.Demo.http): HTTP request file with one request
  per status code for both Minimal API (`/api/items/*`) and controller (`/api/ctrl/items/*`) prefixes.

## Tests layout (`tests/ResultCrafter.Tests/`)

All tests use xUnit v3. Test files mirror the source namespace they cover.

- `Core/` — `ErrorTests`, `ResultVoidTests`, `ResultOfTTests`: primitives, equality, implicit conversions,
  `ErrorType.None` sentinel, `SuccessKind` on void Result, `Map` method.
- `AspNetCore/` — `DependencyInjectionTests`, `HttpErrorCatalogTests`, `ProblemDetailsBuilderTests`,
  `ProblemDetailsEnricherTests`: pipeline wiring, status/title/detail/typeUri mapping, enrichment caching,
  `ErrorType.None` rejection.
- `AspNetCore/Controllers/` — `ControllerResultExtensionsTests` (~37 tests), `ProblemActionResultTests` (~24
  tests using `CapturingProblemDetailsService` spy), `ControllerResultAttributesTests` (~17 tests verifying
  status codes, types, `AllowMultiple`, and `AttributeTargets.Method`).
- `FluentValidation/` — `ValidatorExtensionsTests`: null on success, field-errors dict on failure.
- `MediatR/` — `ResultValidationBehaviorTests`, `VoidResultValidationBehaviorTests`: validator pass-through,
  aggregation, cancellation token flow, and open-behavior registration.

## Extension points and conventions

- To add a new `ErrorType`: add the enum value in `ErrorType.cs`, add a factory on `Error`, add `Status`/`Title`
  /`DefaultDetail`/`TypeUri` cases in `HttpErrorCatalog`, add `ProducesXxx` attribute and builder extension.
- Never add per-request host resolution to `GetInstance` — it must remain a relative URI for proxy safety.
- `x-rc` and `x-rc-error-id` are internal pipeline markers. They are always stripped before the response is
  written. Do not document them as public API.
- Both `IProblemDetailsService.WriteAsync` and `TryWriteAsync` exist. Use `WriteAsync` when a
  writer is guaranteed (i.e. inside ResultCrafter, where `AddResultCrafter()` always calls
  `AddProblemDetails()`). Use `TryWriteAsync` only when a fallback is needed if no writer is
   registered. `WriteAsync` delegates to `TryWriteAsync` internally and throws
   `InvalidOperationException` if no writer handled the context.
## Optional

- [README.md](README.md): Full usage documentation, alternatives comparison, configuration reference, performance
  notes.
- [ResultCrafter.Demo.http](tests/ResultCrafter.Demo/ResultCrafter.Demo.http): Run the demo project then execute
  requests directly in JetBrains Rider, VS Code REST Client, or any `.http`-compatible tool.
